turns.content: i need to vent for a minute because if i dont talk to someone im going to lose it
Go ahead, I'm here. What's going on?

busy season is actually destroying me. i did 14 hours yesterday. FOURTEEN. got in at 7:30, didnt leave till half 9 at night. and then i got home and my manager pinged me on teams at 10pm asking if i could review a working paper before morning. like are you actually having a laugh
14 hours and then a Teams ping at 10pm — that's brutal. Did you respond to the message or leave it till morning?

i did it because im stupid. i sat in bed with my laptop for another 45 minutes reviewing the thing because i knew if i didnt do it hed just chase me at 7am anyway and id be stressed the whole night thinking about it. so basically i worked a 15 hour day. wallah i wanted to cry

That's not stupid — that's the impossible position where both options feel bad. You either do it and lose your evening, or you don't do it and spend the night anxious about it. The real problem isn't your decision, it's that someone thought 10pm on a weeknight was an acceptable time to assign review work.

Is this the norm during busy season, or is this worse than usual?

its the norm tbh. like every year from january to march its like this. the seniors and managers just pile work on and expect you to absorb it because thats what they had to do when they were juniors. its this stupid cycle where nobody questions it because everyone went through it. ive had maybe 2 proper evenings off in the last 3 weeks

The "I suffered through it so you should too" culture is one of the most toxic things about certain industries. It ensures nothing ever improves because questioning the workload gets framed as not being tough enough rather than the system being broken.

2 proper evenings off in 3 weeks is genuinely unsustainable. How are you physically holding up? Are you sleeping enough, eating properly?

loool sleeping. thats funny. i get like 5-6 hours a night if im lucky. eating is... questionable. i had a meal deal for lunch yesterday and then didnt eat dinner because by the time i got home i was too tired to cook and just wanted to sleep. im living off tea and biscuits at my desk basically

Tea and biscuits as a food group is not great long-term. I know cooking feels impossible when you're getting home at 9:30pm, but the lack of proper food is going to make everything harder — your energy, your focus, your mood. It's a downward spiral where being tired makes you eat badly which makes you more tired.

Some minimal-effort options:
- Batch cook on Sunday — even just rice and a stew that lasts 3-4 days
- Keep easy protein sources at work — nuts, protein bars, hummus and pitta
- Overnight oats — literally 2 minutes of prep the night before, grab and go in the morning

I know this sounds like "just eat better" which is annoying when you're exhausted, but even small improvements help.

no youre right and i know youre right. my mum keeps telling me the same thing. she called me yesterday and was like aisha youre going to make yourself ill and i was like hooyo i literally do not have time to breathe let alone cook. she offered to make me food and drop it off which is so sweet but she lives on the other side of manchester and i feel bad making her do that

Your mum offering to drop food off is honestly one of the most loving things — and I'd say let her do it, at least once or twice. Mums generally want to help and saying no to make her life easier can actually make her worry more. If she drops off a big batch of something, that's you sorted for several days.

But I get the guilt of feeling like a burden. Could you meet her halfway — literally? Like she makes the food and you pick it up on the weekend when you have a bit more time?

yeah thats actually what we usually do. i go to hers on sunday for dinner with the family and she always sends me home with like 4 containers of food. somali food keeps well tbh, the rice and stew stuff lasts ages in the fridge. i just ran out quicker this week because i havent had time to go shopping for anything else to supplement it

So the system is actually there — your mum's Sunday food drops are carrying you through the week. You just need to fill in the gaps. Could you do a quick online shop? Tesco or Asda deliver, takes 10 minutes to order, and you can stock up on the easy stuff — bread, eggs, tinned beans, fruit, whatever requires zero effort to prepare. Saves you having to physically go to a shop.

yeah i should just do a delivery order honestly. ive been saying ill go to the shop for like 5 days and it hasnt happened. ill do it tonight actually. get some basics in. its just hard to think about admin stuff like food shopping when your brain is completely fried from staring at spreadsheets for 14 hours. like my capacity for making decisions outside of work is ZERO right now

Decision fatigue is very real — when you've spent all day making analytical decisions at work, even choosing what to buy from Tesco feels like too much. A tip: save a "busy season order" as a favourite on the app. Same basket every week, one click reorder, zero thinking required. Set it up once when you have the energy and it runs on autopilot for the rest of January through March.

When does busy season actually end for you? Is there a light at the end of the tunnel?

mid march usually. sometimes it drags into april if a client is behind on getting us their stuff but normally by late march things start calming down. so ive got like another month of this. which feels like an eternity right now but i know ill get through it because i got through it last year too. i just hate it loool. anyway thanks for letting me vent. i feel slightly less insane now

You got through it last year and you'll get through it again — but do the food delivery order tonight and let your mum feed you. Those two things alone will make the next month more survivable. And if you need to vent again, vent away. One month. You've got this.
